key_risks: AI/semiconductor rebound could cause cap-weighted indexes to outperform this defensive tilt, generating negative alpha vs SPY
Hot June CPI (July 14) or weak jobs report could pressure rate-sensitive financials and broad equities
Healthcare leadership may reverse if EMA catalyst fades or drug-pricing policy headlines emerge
Geopolitical escalation after US strike on Iran could spike volatility and hit cyclicals like industrials and financials

portfolio.rationale: Healthcare led the market on positive EMA regulatory catalysts, has low beta (0.38) and is near 52w highs; defensive with fundamental support amid AI-stock weakness.
Financials and regional banks show broadening leadership with strong benchmark-relative returns; firming yields and steepening curve plus solid GDP/claims data support.
Strong flash manufacturing PMI (59-month high output) and cyclical broadening support industrials; positive 30d alpha and near highs.
Two-thirds of S&P stocks rose while cap-weighted indexes were dragged by AI names; equal-weight captures the broadening and avoids mega-cap semis concentration risk.
Low-vol factor (beta 0.08) is near highs with positive alpha; defensive ballast against AI/tech drawdown risk and a heavy macro data calendar.

portfolio_rationale: Tilt toward defensive and broadening leadership (healthcare, financials, industrials, equal-weight, low-vol) given AI/semiconductor weakness dragging cap-weighted indexes while two-thirds of S&P stocks rose, with low-beta ballast ahead of CPI/jobs/FOMC-minutes catalysts.
rationale_summary: Overweight sectors with current leadership and fundamental catalysts (healthcare regulatory, financials/curve, industrials PMI) plus equal-weight and low-vol to capture market breadth while limiting mega-cap AI drawdown exposure.
